codeReview.summary: The feature adds a substantial BiometricVault implementation and broad unit coverage, but initialize() still performs a second biometric read during provisioning, which contradicts the mission contract for first-launch setup and can throw away the just-created vault on prompt failure.

issues.description: initialize() calls NativeBiometricVault.getSecret() immediately after generateAndStoreSecret(). VAL-VAULT-001 requires provisioning without an unlock prompt, and the contract evidence explicitly expects getSecret not to run during initialize. Because the catch block later deletes the native secret after any post-provision error, canceling or failing this extra prompt discards the just-created vault and forces the user back through setup instead of leaving a provisioned vault to unlock later.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true even though the transcript shows implementation started before the new tests were created or run, which conflicts with mobile-feature-worker's required red→green workflow.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md requires failing tests first. In the transcript skeleton for session 11a782d0-3e30-41fa-a020-eeb1646f36cb, src/lib/enbox/biometric-vault.ts is created before src/lib/enbox/__tests__/biometric-vault.test.ts, and the first scoped biometric-vault test run appears only afterward; the handoff still reports skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: Review failed: initialize() still prompts via getSecret during provisioning, violating the validation contract's no-prompt setup behavior and making setup cancellation destructive.
